result:
Done. Shipped to `main` (commit `c49f0ff`).

## What changed

| Requirement | Implementation |
|---|---|
| **DB-level uniqueness** | New `word_index (word_bg TEXT PRIMARY KEY, heap_id UUID)` table in `migrate/route.ts`. The PK enforces that no Cyrillic word can belong to two heaps. Backfilled from existing heaps on migrate (one owner per word, `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`). |
| **Seed guard** | New `lib/seed.ts` ‚Üí `insertHeapsDeduped()`. Loads all existing `bg` words, skips any word already in the DB *or* earlier in the same batch, inserts kept words into `word_index`, and skips heaps that end up empty. Wired into all four seed endpoints (`seed`, `seed-extra`, `seed-map2`, `seed-map3`). Each response now returns `skipped` (count) + `skippedWords` (list). |
| **Backfill report** | New `/api/db/dedup-check` endpoint reports cross-heap duplicates (no deletion). I also ran a static scan of the seed data ‚ÄĒ see below. |
| **Build** | `npm run build` ‚úď compiled clean, no errors/warnings. |
| **Commit + push** | `"Dedup: unique word constraint + seed guard"` pushed to `main`. |

## Dedup report (static scan of seed data)

375 word entries ‚Üí **360 unique, 15 duplicates**. All collisions are Map 3 (and one Map 2) re-using words from earlier maps:

`–ī–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā, —É—ā—Ä–Ķ, –≤—á–Ķ—Ä–į, —Ö–ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ, –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—ā, —Ź–ľ, —Ā–Ņ—Ź` (Map 1‚ÜĒ3), `–Ī–ĺ–Ľ–Ĺ–ł—Ü–į, –Ī–į–Ĺ–ļ–į, –Ľ–Ķ–≥–Ľ–ĺ, –ľ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ, –Ņ–į—Ä–ļ, —Ä–į–Ī–ĺ—ā—Ź, –ļ—Ä–į—Ā–ł–≤` (extra‚ÜĒ3), `–Ņ–ĺ–Ľ–ł—Ü–ł—Ź` (Map 2‚ÜĒ3).

With the guard in place, re-seeding would now skip these 15 and report them in the response. Existing data is untouched, per the requirement.

Note: as instructed, this is code-only ‚ÄĒ I did **not** run migrate/seed/dedup-check against the production DB. To activate the constraint you'll need to run the migrate endpoint (creates `word_index` + backfills) once on prod.
